Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Calm & lift your mood • The ten-root tonic

Dashamoola Bedtime Decoction

The classic ten-root Ayurvedic postpartum tonic — simmered small, taken warm in the evening. Tradition, honestly labelled.
Makes 1 small cup (30–60 ml)
Time 15 min
Caffeine none
For you · after the last feed
When to drink Evening, after the last feed — a small warm cup (30–60 ml), once or twice a day at most.

Why this heals you

  • Ten roots — the classic Ayurvedic formula for the 42-day postpartum window tradition
  • Vata-settling — traditionally taken to ground a wired-but-tired nervous system tradition
  • Warming — used for strength rebuild and easing aches after birth tradition
  • Warm milk & ghee — the classic evening carrier for the bitter roots tradition
Ingredients
1–2 tspDashamoola powder (your jar — the powder, not the arishta)
½ cup / 120 mlWater
½ cupWarm milk (optional · the bedtime way)
½ tspGhee (optional)
1 tspHoney (optional · stir in off the heat)
What dashamoola is

Dashamoola = “ten roots” — a single blended powder of ten root herbs (bilva, agnimantha, shyonaka, patala, gambhari + the five “lesser” roots), ground together as one classical formula.

It tastes earthy and bitter — that’s right. Milk, ghee and a little honey are the traditional way to soften it.

Method
1
Simmer down

Stir 1–2 tsp dashamoola powder into 120 ml (½ cup) water in a small pan. Simmer gently, uncovered, 10–12 min, until reduced by about half.

2
Strain small

Strain through a fine sieve into a small cup — you want 30–60 ml of dark decoction. That cordial-sized cup is the dose: 1–2× a day, no more.

3
Make it bedtime

Top up with ½ cup warm milk and ½ tsp ghee if you like; stir in 1 tsp honey off the heat.

4
Sip, small & warm

Sip warm in the evening, after the last feed. A second cup is fine earlier in the day — the same small 30–60 ml pour, made fresh each time.

Just for you, Mum

An evening ritual straight from the Ayurvedic 42-day window — ten roots simmered small to settle the day while your body knits itself back together. Brew it while the last feed winds down; it holds warm in the pan until you’re ready. Centuries of mothers took it exactly like this; we label that honestly: tradition.

Good to know
The formUse the powder, not the arishta bf note — the common bottled form, Dashmularishta, is fermented and contains self-generated alcohol. Your powder decoction is the right form while nursing.
EvidenceCenturies of traditional postpartum use; not yet studied in modern trials — we say so plainly.
Keep it culinary1–2 tsp simmered to a 30–60 ml cup, 1–2× a day — a kitchen dose, not a supplement megadose.
Why we say this
Traditional — dashamoola is core Ayurvedic postpartum canon (no single author); this cup follows the classical decoction method using your powder. Traditional-use reference: Purusha Ayurveda on dashamoola postpartum. Every claim on this card is tradition, not trial evidence — and no milk-supply claim is made.
